summary: knowledge impact moves the citation-join out of SQL (instr(cs.raw_json, kld.url)) into Go using parsed-JSON URL extraction and exact match.
reason: The original SQL used the same raw-blob substring search that PATCH(greptile-9) replaced in visibility traffic-citations. A doc URL like https://example.com/blog would prefix-match unrelated citation URLs (.../blog/post-extended) and also match its own string appearing inside an image src / canonical href / author link embedded in the snapshot blob — both paths inflated cited_doc_count and total_cites, making the 'is this library worth maintaining?' answer unreliable. Fix: pull libraries, library documents, and citation snapshots in three cheap queries; parse each snapshot once into a normalized URL set; exact-match per-library doc URLs against the snapshot's URL set in Go. Same public method signature; identical KnowledgeImpactRow shape.
